title: search folder name not the connections
author: hungrysnake
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
i have over 100 connections and search option is the only way can i locate the conneciton quickly.
however, i must name every single one of them to be searchable (include all the keywords that I can search in the connection name)


## Expected Behavior
<!--- If you're describing a bug, tell us what should happen -->
<!--- If you're suggesting a change/improvement, tell us how it should work -->
I want have a way to searc...

iterations.description: The current connection tree search functionality does not include child connections when their parent folder matches the search criteria, leading to an incomplete display of search results.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Triage refresh (P4 version-label debt): this issue is tagged with legacy version labels. Please retest on the latest build (fork v1.79.x or upstream dev), then share reproducible steps and expected vs actual behavior. If still valid, keep open and relabel to current triage scope (for example Need 2 check). If fixed, close with resolution.

Current legacy version labels: 1.77.3

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The current connection tree search functionality does not include child connections when their parent folder matches the search criteria, leading to an incomplete display of search results.
Approach: Refactor `ConnectionTreeSearchTextFilter` to pre-calculate a set of all `ConnectionInfo` objects that should be visible based on the filter text. This set will include: 1) objects directly matching the filter criteria; 2) all descendants of a matching `ContainerInfo`; and 3) all ancestors of any matching `ConnectionInfo` or `ContainerInfo`. The `Filter` method will then simply check for presence in this pre-calculated set.
